archimate:ApplicationInterface.name: ERP System
archimate:ApplicationInterface.documentation:
Definition: An Application Interface in an ERP system refers to the set of protocols, methods, and tools that enable communication and interaction between the ERP system and other applications or systems. It allows data and functionalities to be exchanged between the ERP system and external systems, such as CRM (Customer Relationship Management) systems, HR (Human Resources) systems, supply chain management systems, or third-party applications. The Application Interface facilitates seamless integration, data synchronization, and workflow coordination, enabling efficient information flow and process automation across the organization's ecosystem of systems.
Source: Oracle
Source reference: https://www.oracle.com/erp/what-is-erp-integration/
LOST view: TVA-Functional Architecture Principles
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/ERPSystemApplicationInterface
ABB name: egovera:ERPSystemApplicationInterface
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/ERPSystemApplicationInterface
Interoperability Layer: TechnicalApplication
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/WebServiceApplicationInterface

properties.value: An Application Interface in an ERP system refers to the set of protocols, methods, and tools that enable communication and interaction between the ERP system and other applications or systems. It allows data and functionalities to be exchanged between the ERP system and external systems, such as CRM (Customer Relationship Management) systems, HR (Human Resources) systems, supply chain management systems, or third-party applications. The Application Interface facilitates seamless integration, data synchronization, and workflow coordination, enabling efficient information flow and process automation across the organization's ecosystem of systems.
